title: Chromolaena squarrulosa
taxonomy: Chromolaena squarrulosa is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It belongs to the genus Chromolaena, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Chromolaena squarrulosa is a shrub or small tree that is native to Central America and the Caribbean. The species is closely related to other members of the genus, including Chromolaena odorata and Chromolaena nervosa. Chromolaena squarrulosa is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae and the genus Chromolaena, with the full classification being Chromolaena squarrulosa (S.F. Blake) R.M. King & H.R. Robinson.
uses: Chromolaena squarrulosa is a plant that has been traditionally used in various parts of the world for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating wounds and infections. In some cultures, it is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, Chromolaena squarrulosa is believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help in preventing the spread of infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of the plant's medicinal properties.

intro: Chromolaena squarrulosa, also known as Spanish needle or tree verbena, is a perennial plant native to tropical regions, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Chromolaena squarrulosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk remedies and modern supplements to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
